name: Superior Savings Bank
description_short: Community bank chartered in 1924 offering personal banking, mortgages, mobile banking, and ATM access through QwikBank locations in Douglas County, Wisconsin.
description_long:
Superior Savings Bank was originally chartered in 1924 as "State Building – Loan and Savings Association" with the primary mission of serving the community with residential real estate loans and providing a safe deposit location for local residents. The institution converted to its current charter as Superior Savings Bank on January 1, 1993, and has maintained its original headquarters at 1130 Tower Avenue in Superior, Wisconsin since 1960. As the longest-standing community bank in Douglas County, the institution has built its reputation on deep local roots and personalized service.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), investment products, personal loans, and mortgage financing. Their mortgage services include home purchase loans, construction loans, and refinancing options. Customers can access accounts through online banking, mobile banking applications, e-statements available 24/7, and a network of QwikBank ATMs that feature full-service deposit capabilities. The bank partners with established lending programs including WHEDA (Wisconsin Housing and Economic Development Authority) and the Federal Home Loan Bank's Downpayment Plus program to expand homeownership access.

What distinguishes Superior Savings Bank is its positioning as a community-focused institution with 100+ years of local presence and commitment to Douglas County residents. The bank actively invests in modern banking technology (mobile banking, e-statements, accessibility features) while maintaining personalized service orientation. Their partnership with established state and federal lending programs demonstrates commitment to expanding homeownership access for income-eligible buyers, including down payment assistance programs.

As a traditional community bank, Superior Savings Bank is best suited for customers seeking local relationship banking with modern conveniences. The website provides basic product information but lacks detailed rate disclosures, fee schedules, or product comparisons. Prospective customers will need to contact the bank directly or visit a physical location for comprehensive pricing and eligibility information on specific products. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking portal with bill pay
QwikBank ATM network with deposit capability
pros: Longest-standing community bank in Douglas County with 100+ years of established presence and local commitment
Offers mortgage products with partnerships to WHEDA and Downpayment Plus programs for income-eligible first-time homebuyers
Full-suite digital banking including 24/7 mobile banking, e-statements, and online account management
QwikBank ATM network with full deposit capability for convenient after-hours access
Member FDIC and Equal Housing Lender certification providing deposit protection and fair lending assurance
Accessible website design with AudioEye accessibility toolbar for customers with visual impairments
Personal loan options and certificate of deposit (CD) products for savings goals
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted primarily to Douglas County, Wisconsin — not available to customers in most other states
Website lacks current interest rates, fee schedules, and product comparison details requiring customers to contact bank directly
No clear disclosure of minimum account balances, opening deposit requirements, or qualification criteria for personal loans
No mention of business banking services despite being chartered as a full commercial bank entity
best_for: Douglas County, Wisconsin residents seeking personalized community banking with local decision-making
First-time homebuyers and existing homeowners interested in WHEDA or down payment assistance programs
Customers prioritizing relationship banking and local ATM access over online-only banking convenience
Individuals seeking a traditional bank with modern digital capabilities and accessibility features
diagnosis: Superior Savings Bank is best for residents of Douglas County, Wisconsin seeking traditional community banking with modern digital conveniences and personalized mortgage service. The primary caveat is severe geographic limitation — the bank operates only in Douglas County and is not available to customers outside this region, making it unsuitable for those seeking multi-state banking access.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
